service: 3 Engage residents and connect them to best-in-class social services.
oneLiner: Because NYCHA houses hundreds of thousands of New Yorkers directly, it has taken on a social-services connector role beyond just being a landlord — a natural extension of its 1934 founding mission to address the Depression-era housing crisis holistically, not just with apartments.
summary: As the first public housing authority in the country (created 1934), NYCHA houses a large share of New York's lowest-income residents in one place, which has made it a natural touchpoint for connecting tenants to other social services — employment programs, health resources, and community programs — beyond simply maintaining buildings. This resident-services role has grown alongside NYCHA's core landlord function as the needs of its tenant population have evolved.

sources.label: New York City Housing Authority — Wikipedia
